ClaudeReviewer.docs: Claude-backed reviewer implementing the Reviewer usecase port. Spawns `claude -p --bare --permission-mode dontAsk --allowedTools Read Grep Glob 'Bash(git diff:*)' 'Bash(git show:*)' 'Bash(git log:*)' 'Bash(git ls-files:*)' --disallowedTools Edit Write --output-format json --json-schema '<REVIEW_OUTPUT_SCHEMA_JSON>' --model <model> <prompt>` as a subprocess, reads the verdict from stdout envelope's `structured_output` field, and passes it to `parse_review_final_message`. Read-only for the reviewer role is best-effort and permission-based: `claude -p` has no sandbox flag (no kernel-level sandbox equivalent to `codex exec --sandbox read-only`), so this is NOT structurally enforced at the OS level and is NOT symmetric with CodexReviewer's `--sandbox read-only`. The flags used (CN-01): `--bare` skips hooks / MCP / CLAUDE.md auto-detection (does not guarantee that host `permissions.allow` is ignored); `--permission-mode dontAsk` auto-denies tool calls not on the allow list, but actions already registered in the host's `permissions.allow` remain active; `--disallowedTools Edit Write` removes Edit and Write from the model's context (defense-in-depth); `--allowedTools Read Grep Glob Bash(git diff:*) Bash(git show:*) Bash(git log:*) Bash(git ls-files:*)` pre-approves the read-only inspection set. In a standard environment (host `permissions.allow` does not grant write operations) write/edit tools will be denied, but host settings could in principle broaden the permitted surface. Fields are all private (has_stripped_fields: true). `new` uses named generics `M: Into<String>` and `B: Into<String>` so each parameter has a distinct type variable. (IN-01 / CN-01 / CN-05 / CN-06 / AC-02)

ReviewV2CompositionWithClaude.docs: All v2 adapters needed for actual review (ClaudeReviewer). Fields are intentionally private — callers access behaviour through the string-accepting free functions in this module (`run_claude_review_str`, etc.) so that concrete infrastructure types (`ReviewCycle`, `FsReviewStore`, …) are not exposed to the CLI layer (IN-02 / CN-02 / AC-03). Parallel to ReviewV2CompositionWithCodex.

infrastructure::review_v2::cli_compositi.docs: Builds the v2 review composition with a real ClaudeReviewer. Returns Err on discovery/config/store failures. Parallel to build_review_v2_with_reviewer. (IN-02 / CN-02 / AC-03)

String-accepting variant of `build_review_v2_with_claude_reviewer`. Converts `track_id_str` to `TrackId` and delegates. Callers that must not import `domain::TrackId` use this entry point (IN-02 / CN-02 / AC-03).

Runs the full Claude review cycle from string inputs. Mirrors run_codex_review_str with ClaudeReviewer in place of CodexReviewer. Encapsulates TrackId, ScopeName, RoundType, ReviewOutcome, Verdict, FastVerdict, ReviewWriter, and ReviewerFinding conversions so the CLI layer never imports these domain types directly (IN-02 / CN-02 / AC-03).
